lines.en_lit: And from where please? It is stated here: Atonement, and it is stated there, at Horeb: atonement Just as there, “Please, so too here, please And from where that the name It is stated here: Atonement, and it is stated with regard to the heifer whose neck is broken: “Atone, Just as there, the name so too here, the name

Abaye said: Granted, Horeb cannot be derived from the heifer whose neck is broken, what was, was. However, you derive heifer whose neck is broken from Horeb, And if you say that is so, didn’t we learn the priests say: “Atone, for Your nation, Israel” while does not state please. difficult

And respond after he It was taught Rabbi says “When I call out the name of the Lord, give glory to our God” Moses said to the Jewish people: When I mention the name of the Holy One, Blessed be He, you give glory Ḥananya, son of the brother of Rabbi Yehoshua, says “The memory of the righteous shall be for a blessing” The prophet, said to the Jewish people: When I mention the Righteous One of all worlds, you accord Him a blessing.

MISHNA: came to eastern of the courtyard, to north of the altar. The deputy to his right, and the head of the patrilineal family to his left. And two goats there, and there was a receptacle there, and in it were two lots. These were boxwood, ben Gamla fashioned them of gold, would mention him favorably
ben Katin made twelve spigots for the basin as had only two. He also made a machine [mukheni] for the basin so that its water would not be disqualified by remaining overnight.

King Munbaz would make the handles of all the Yom Kippur vessels of gold. Helene, his mother, fashioned a gold chandelier above the entrance of the Sanctuary. She also fashioned a golden tablet [tavla] on which the portion sota was written. Nicanor, miracles were performed to his doors, And would mention those favorably.

GEMARA: From that it says to north of the altar, by inference that altar does not stand in the north whose It is Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov, as it was taught northward before God” that entire north be vacant. the statement of Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov.
But isn’t the first clause, Rabbi Elazar, son of Rabbi Shimon, entire is Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov. and teach adjacent to between the Entrance Hall and the altar

the deputy to the right and the head of the patrilineal family is to his left. Rav Yehuda said One who walks to the right of his teacher is a boor, we learn the deputy, is to the right and the head of the patrilineal family is to his left?

And furthermore, it was taught Three who were walking on the road the teacher in the middle, the greater on his right, and the lesser to his left. And so too do we find with the three ministering angels who came to Abraham: Michael, in the middle, Gabriel was to his right, and Raphael was to his left.

Rav Shmuel bar Pappa interpreted it before Rav Adda: so that he is obscured by his teacher. But wasn’t it taught One who walks next to his teacher is a boor; behind his teacher, is among the arrogant. to side
And there was receptacle there, and in it were two lots. The Sages taught “And Aaron shall place lots on the two goats, Lots from any material,

might he place two on this and two on that the verse states: “One lot for God and one lot for Azazel” there is only one lot here for God, and there is only one lot here for Azazel. might he place of God and of Azazel on this and of God and of Azazel on that the verse states: One lot for God, there is only one here for God, and there is only one here for Azazel. If so, what the verse states lots, that be identical; he should not make one of gold and one of silver, one big and one small.

lots from any material. obvious, No, necessary which was taught Because we find with regard to the frontplate that the name of God was written upon it and it was made of gold, might that this too, the verse states: Lot, lot, to include It includes olive includes walnut includes boxwood.

ben Katin made twelve spigots for the basin. taught: so that twelve of his fellow priests, who are occupied with the daily offering sanctify their hands and their feet simultaneously. taught: In the morning, is full, one sanctifies his hands and his feet from at the top afternoon, when is low, he sanctifies his hands and his feet at the bottom.
He also made a machine for the basin. What machine? Abaye said: a wheel with which he lowered
King Munbaz would make the handles of all the vessels he should have made themselves of gold,
